food_subgroup: Japanese bento set meal — sushi and cooked components

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002809 covers bento as a food product category; this record is a specific children's bento variant with tamagoyaki, crab salad, corn salad gunkan, and cucumber roll components. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers composite mixed dishes; no specific bento child code exists. Closest parent used for this multi-component Japanese children's bento.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to a children's bento meal set. SNOMED CT not applicable for this food record.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Kids Bento box as served at Sushi Express Singapore, estimated at approximately 280 g, comprising tamagoyaki slices, crab salad, corn salad gunkan, and cucumber roll pieces.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Composite estimation from sushi rice, tamagoyaki, surimi crab salad, and cucumber roll components; cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entries for Japanese sushi components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Sushi Express Kids Bento exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component composition: approximately 45% sushi rice (USDA FDC #169752), 20% tamagoyaki/egg omelette (USDA FDC #172185), 20% surimi-based crab salad with mayonnaise (USDA FDC #175168 surimi + #172345 mayonnaise), 10% corn salad gunkan (USDA FDC #169998 corn), and 5% cucumber (USDA FDC #168409). Values are per 100 g of the combined bento. Confidence is medium due to estimation. Vitamin D and selenium are not consistently reported across these component entries and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — sushi rice and mixed Japanese rice-based meal analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Sushi Express Kids Bento. GI of 52 is estimated using sushi rice as the primary glycemic driver (GI ~52 per University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2021, white sushi rice entry). The bento's protein and fat components (egg, surimi, mayonnaise) lower the overall glycemic response relative to plain rice. GL per serving calculated as: (52 × 21.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 2.80 serving factor) / 100 = approximately 31.7. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended for clinical use.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g; a full bento serving (~280 g) delivers approximately 868 mg sodium, representing 37–43% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit portion size to half a bento box and avoid adding soy sauce, as the sushi rice seasoning and surimi already contribute significant sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sushi rice has a GI of approximately 52 and the full bento delivers an estimated glycemic load of ~31.7, which is high; the protein and fat from egg and surimi partially attenuate postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this bento in a reduced portion (half serving) and pair it with additional non-starchy vegetables to lower the overall glycemic load and blunt postprandial blood glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~0.9 g/100 g) from egg yolk and mayonnaise, combined with elevated sodium, presents a compounded cardiovascular risk when consumed in full-serving quantities.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose this bento occasionally rather than regularly, request reduced-mayonnaise preparation where possible, and avoid adding soy sauce to keep sodium within cardioprotective limits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Kids Bento at Sushi Express Singapore is a children's Japanese set meal served in a single bento box, priced at SGD 8.00. It comprises four components: tamagoyaki (a lightly sweetened Japanese rolled omelette made from layered pan-fried egg), crab salad (surimi-based imitation crab mixed with Japanese mayonnaise), corn salad gunkan (a boat-shaped nori-wrapped sushi topped with sweet corn and mayonnaise), and a cucumber roll (kappa maki — vinegared sushi rice rolled with fresh cucumber in nori). The overall flavour profile is mild, slightly sweet, and umami-forward, making it accessible to young palates. Texturally, the bento combines the soft chewiness of vinegared sushi rice, the silky egg of tamagoyaki, the creamy crab salad, and the crisp freshness of cucumber. Nutritionally, the bento is a moderate-energy mixed meal (~148 kcal per 100 g, ~414 kcal per full serving) providing balanced macronutrients: carbohydrates primarily from sushi rice, protein from egg and surimi, and fat from mayonnaise and egg yolk. Sodium is the primary clinical concern at approximately 868 mg per full serving. The glycemic load is moderate-to-high (~31.7 per serving) due to sushi rice, which is relevant for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes. The bento is not suitable for halal-certified diets without verification, contains egg and fish allergens, and is not vegetarian due to surimi. It is a popular introductory Japanese meal for children in Singapore's fast-casual sushi restaurant segment.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Sushi Express Kids Bento exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component composition using USDA FDC SR Legacy entries for sushi rice, tamagoyaki, surimi, mayonnaise, corn, and cucumber. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database sushi rice analogue. Confidence is medium due to estimation methodology and absence of a direct laboratory analysis or approved-source exact match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component USDA FDC source entries used for this estimated composite; vitamin D data is absent from USDA FDC entries for sushi rice (#169752), surimi (#175168), and corn (#169998), and cannot be defensibly assigned without direct analysis. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as selenium content is not reported in the USDA FDC entries for sushi rice (#169752), tamagoyaki/egg (#172185), and cucumber (#168409) used for this estimation; selenium is therefore not calculable from the available approved-source component data. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed negligible/trace for the component foods (egg, surimi, rice, cucumber, corn) per USDA FDC source data — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central component entries. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on sushi rice (University of Sydney GI Database); direct GI measurement of this specific bento product is recommended for clinical applications. Serving size of 280 g is an estimate based on typical Sushi Express bento box portion weights; actual weight may vary. Halal status is unconfirmed — Sushi Express Singapore is not listed as halal-certified by MUIS as of the accessed date; is_halal is set to false pending verification. The food_id GD001935 is from the GrabFood Singapore seed record series and is not present in the FD series food_index.json. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FDC component entries and carry medium confidence.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from seed input. Estimated nutrients via USDA FDC component weighting. Added GI from University of Sydney GI Database sushi rice analogue. Added ontology with closest_parent codes and gap notes. Added Japanese and Chinese local names. Added health context for all three required conditions. Added comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language.

Iteration 2: Addressed validator feedback. Added Malay ('Bento Kanak-kanak', 'Bento Anak-anak'), Tamil ('குழந்தைகள் பென்டோ'), and Hindi ('बच्चों का बेंटो') names to local_names, aliases_by_language, and the flat aliases list to satisfy G6 and Q1/Q8. Added documentation for -1 sentinel values and 0.0 for trans_fat_g in notes to resolve G3.

Iteration 3: Addressed validator feedback for g3_numeric_hygiene. Expanded notes field to explicitly document that vitamin_d_mcg = -1 because vitamin D is not reported in the USDA FDC source entries for sushi rice (#169752), surimi (#175168), and corn (#169998) used in this composite estimation. Explicitly documented that selenium_mcg = -1 because selenium is not reported in the USDA FDC entries for sushi rice (#169752), tamagoyaki/egg (#172185), and cucumber (#168409). Also added cross-reference to nutrient_source.source_match_notes to reinforce the -1 basis. All -1 sentinel values now have explicit missing-data basis documented in notes per G3 requirements.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
